$comment: RESOLUTION MEMORY for ambiguous/divergent hook mappings. sync.py consults this BEFORE prompting: any ambiguity whose id is recorded here is auto-applied (seamless re-sync). New/changed ambiguities are surfaced for interactive resolution and then appended here. Keys: 'role:<role>' for cross-agent divergence on a lifecycle role; 'type:<illegal-type>' for a referenced type that failed the contract. Do not delete entries unless you intend to re-open that decision.

role:session_start.rationale: Operator chose to evolve session events under the agent domain. agent.session.started is a legal 5-token type (domain=agent, entity=session, action=started) using already-allowlisted tokens — no contract regex/domain change needed, unlike the bare session.* form. Supersedes cli.session.* for agent CLIs.

role:post_tool.rationale: The single post-tool hook fires after execution and carries outcome/result, so agent.tool.completed is the accurate, outcome-bearing event the other two agents already use. Claude moves off agent.tool.invoked for cross-agent consistency.

diverged.claude: bloodbank.v1.agent.delegation.completed (delegation entity not allowlisted, contract-illegal hand-edit)

role:subagent_stop.rationale: delegation is not an allowed entity and has no schema. Sub-agent runs are nested invocations per §14, so agent.invocation.completed is canonical. Claude reverts off the illegal delegation type.
